creator: greg@fqdn.com
text:
Machine worked fine for a few weeks,  machine bacame unreachable.  Console was full of
"Oct  6 18:13:08 vortex /kernel: kernel trap 19 with interrupts disabled
Oct  6 18:13:09 vortex /kernel: NMI ISA 2c, EISA ff" type messages.

upon reboot the machine would panic after the first prompt let go.  (f1). I didn't jot down the panic message.  sory.

here is a copy of the kernel log.

Fix: 

I removed half my memory (later confirmed it wasn't bad)
removed ISA sound card,  SB16.
removed TV card.  (happauge winTV)
disconnected IDE CDROM.

ALL these devices worked when I later put them into my dual boot linux/w2k machine on both sides.
I don't know if this is a bug,  but I thought you may of been intrested to hear this.
The machine seems to be working fine now.
How-To-Repeat: it would happen on ever reboot attempt.
